title: Addition of Two Numbers Using Structure

content.problemStatement:
You are required to perform the addition of two numbers using **structures** in C.

Write a program that defines a structure named `Numbers` having two integer members.

**Pass the whole structure** to a function that calculates and returns the **sum** of the two numbers.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer `a`.

The second line contains an integer `b`.



Output Format
Print the sum of the two numbers.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two integers a and b separated by space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of a and b on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The sum of 5 and 7 is 12.
There is one test case. The sum of 10 and 20 is 30.
There is one test case. The sum of 100 and 200 is 300.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read the two integers a and b, compute their sum and output it. The operation is O(1) per test case, so the total complexity is linear in T. No additional data structures are needed.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int a=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int b=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());sb.append(a+b);if(t<T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
